target.title: fix(server): skip tool execution for cancelled responses
target.body:
## Summary

- `handleResponseDone` never checked `response.status`. With `turn_detection.interrupt_response: true`, a barge-in can cancel a response that's still mid function-call generation — but the cancelled `response.done` event can still carry a completed `function_call` in `output`. Previously this would still run `query_agent`/`save_call_report` and queue a follow-up `response.create`, making the bot act and speak right after being interrupted.
- Fix: `handleResponseDone` now returns early when `response.status === "cancelled"`, skipping all tool execution and follow-up responses for that response.

## Test plan

- [x] New regression test `test/server-cancelled-response.test.ts`: emits a `response.done` with `status: "cancelled"` and completed `query_agent`/`save_call_report` function calls, asserts no `conversation.item.create`, no `response.create`, and no `report.json` under the call's actual logger directory.
- [x] `pnpm test` — 46/46 pass
- [x] `pnpm run lint` and `pnpm run typecheck` clean

## Real behavior proof

Ran a real `vox serve` process against a real Twilio-protocol caller and an OpenAI Realtime stand-in (`OPENAI_REALTIME_URL` pointed at a local `ws` server), built at the fix commit (`cbf03dd`).

The stand-in completes the session handshake, then sends a `response.done` with `status: "cancelled"` carrying two completed `function_call` outputs (`query_agent` and `save_call_report`).

Provider-side log (redacted, audio-append spam removed):

```
2026-06-15T11:11:02.649Z provider: realtime stand-in listening on ws://127.0.0.1:8082
2026-06-15T11:11:31.737Z provider: vox connected
2026-06-15T11:11:31.738Z provider: <- session.update {...}
2026-06-15T11:11:32.240Z provider: -> response.done (status: cancelled, function_call x2)
```

vox's own `events.jsonl` for the call (event-type timeline, media frames elided):

```
2026-06-15T11:11:31.732Z [vox]    twilio.ws.connected
2026-06-15T11:11:31.737Z [twilio] connected
2026-06-15T11:11:31.737Z [twilio] start
2026-06-15T11:11:31.737Z [vox]    twilio.start
2026-06-15T11:11:31.738Z [openai] session.created
2026-06-15T11:11:31.739Z [openai] session.updated
2026-06-15T11:11:32.241Z [openai] response.done   <-- status: cancelled, 2 function_calls
2026-06-15T11:11:34.235Z [twilio] stop
2026-06-15T11:11:34.235Z [vox]    twilio.stop
```

After `response.done` (cancelled), vox sends **nothing** back to the realtime provider (no `conversation.item.create`, no `response.create`) and writes **no `report.json`**. The call continues normally for the remaining ~2s and `stop` is handled cleanly; `GET /health` returns `{"ok":true}` afterward.
target.author: joevenner
